caption: Tee time at 7:30 and the front nine was still in shadow. Hard to beat a quiet course before the heat finds it.
Lily found the koi pond at the Desert Botanical Garden and that was the whole afternoon. No complaints from me.
Owen in the cockpit sim at the museum. He held the yoke like he meant it. Brett got the photo before the grin faded.
Sunrise over Camelback before the morning walk Judith makes me take. She's right, of course. Worth the early start.
Down at the Aviation Heritage Museum this morning. The restored P-51 finally has its placards up — worth the wait.
Walked 18 with Tom and Walt before the heat. Shot an 84. Walt is still talking about the one putt he made.
Judith and I made the drive up to Sedona for the day. Red rock never gets old.
School group came through the museum today. Nothing beats a kid asking how a jet engine actually works.
Owen's first time on a real ramp tour. He wanted to push every button. Brett kept a close eye.
Coffee, the morning paper, and a quiet patio. Some mornings the schedule is exactly nothing.
Took the 4Runner out to a viewpoint Walt swears by. He was right. Worth the dirt road.
Kelsey and Daniel flew in from San Diego for the weekend. Good to have a full table again.
New arrivals in the docent reading pile. Started the Connelly first — old habits.
First round of the new year. Cold start, clear skies, and Tom still lost three balls. Some things hold.
Whole family under one roof for the holiday. Owen, Lily, and a kitchen that didn't sit still all day.
Museum's holiday open house drew a good crowd. The cockpit sim line never got shorter.
Thanksgiving with Judith's pie and a quiet house the next morning. Grateful, and a little full.
Ed was in town — a long lunch and the same old stories we always come back to. Good company.
Restoration crew got the radial engine turning over for the first time in years. That sound still gets me.
Visited Brett's place in Denver. Owen wanted to show me every model plane on the shelf. We made it through most.
Tee time at 7:30 and the front nine was still in shadow. Hard to beat a quiet course before the heat finds it.
Red rocks at Sedona doing their thing at sunset. Judith says this one's going on the wall.
4Runner loaded, cooler packed, north on the 17. Flagstaff by lunch.
Owen sent me his chess trophy over FaceTime. Prouder than I let on.
Jazz on the patio, glass of red, the desert cooling off. This is the reward.
Well-struck 9-iron on the par 3. The Titleist and I have an understanding.
North Rim of the Grand Canyon. Twenty-two thousand hours in the air and this still stops me cold.
Docent morning at the museum. The simulator exhibit had a full house of fifth graders.
Lily found the patio dinosaur again. It lives here now, apparently.
Troon North in the early light. Walked all eighteen and felt every one of them, in a good way.
Judith's watercolor of the Superstitions. Better than the photo I took beside it.
Petrified Forest on a clear cool morning. The kind of quiet you drive four hours for.
Clubhouse lunch with Tom after the round. He's still claiming that putt was luck.
Classic rock in the 4Runner, windows down, open highway to Bryce. Eagles on repeat.
Restored B-17 out on the museum floor. Ran three tours and could have run three more.
Steak night, the one Judith allows. Green chile on everything, doctor's orders be damned.
Sunrise over the fairway, sprinklers still running. Six-thirty blue is my favorite color.
Zion narrows from the overlook. Adding it to the list of places we'll come back to.
Ed brought an old crew photo to dinner. Tokyo layovers, younger men, same laugh.
Grandkids' voices on the big screen Sunday. Best forty-five minutes of the week.
Practice range at dawn before the group shows up. Just me and a bucket of balls.
Sedona trail with Judith, easy pace, big views. Red Rock Lodge patio waiting for us.
Miles Davis and a cool evening. Kind of Blue is a whole mood on the patio.
New Michael Connelly on the nightstand. Bosch never lets me sleep early.
Saturday group at We-Ko-Pa. Walt narrated every swing. Still shot my number.
